sections.title: Overview
sections.content: A mucosal atomisation device delivers selected medications across the nasal mucosa when IV access is unnecessary, delayed, or undesirable. It is particularly useful for analgesia, seizure control, and naloxone delivery when a small, concentrated dose can be absorbed effectively.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.content:
1. Draw up the prescribed dose and attach the atomiser tip securely

2. Position the head so the nostrils are accessible without compromising airway management

3. Place the nozzle just inside the nostril and aim laterally rather than directly at the septum

4. Deliver the dose with a brisk push to create a fine mist rather than a slow stream

5. Split larger doses between nostrils to improve mucosal coverage and absorption

6. Reassess for clinical effect and the need for alternative routes if the response is poor

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Nasal trauma, heavy epistaxis, marked congestion, or large drug volumes can reduce absorption and should prompt consideration of another route
- Highly concentrated solutions are preferred because smaller volumes absorb more reliably
- Reassess for ventilation, sedation level, seizure control, or analgesic effect according to the drug used
- Document the drug, dose, nostril distribution, and response
